legal.privacyP1: TRS ("we", "us") respects your privacy. This policy describes how we collect, use, and protect information when you use our website and services.
legal.privacyP2: We may collect information you provide (such as name, email, and message content) when you contact us or use our forms. We use this information to respond to inquiries and improve our services.
legal.privacyP3: We do not sell your personal data. We may use trusted service providers who process data on our behalf under appropriate agreements.
legal.privacyP4: You may request access, correction, or deletion of your personal data where applicable by contacting us at the email below.
legal.termsP1: By accessing this website, you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the site.
legal.termsP2: Content on this site is for general information. We strive for accuracy but make no warranties regarding completeness or suitability for a particular purpose.
legal.termsP3: You agree not to misuse the site, attempt unauthorized access, or interfere with its operation.
legal.termsP4: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.

about.intro: TRS is a full-service digital agency passionate about creating exceptional web experiences. Since 2016, we've helped businesses of all sizes transform their digital presence and achieve their goals through innovative technology and creative design.

values.description: Every project starts with understanding your goals. We align our work with your business objectives to deliver meaningful impact.

values.description: We take pride in crafting exceptional digital experiences. No shortcuts, no compromises — just outstanding work.

values.title: Innovation
values.description: We stay at the cutting edge of technology and design trends to bring you solutions that are ahead of the curve.

values.title: Collaboration
values.description: Your project is a partnership. We work closely with you at every step to ensure the result exceeds expectations.

blogPost.introP: The web development landscape continues to evolve at an unprecedented pace. As we navigate through 2026, several key trends are reshaping how we build, deploy, and maintain web applications. Let's explore the most impactful developments that every developer and business should be aware of.
blogPost.aiP: Artificial intelligence has fundamentally transformed the development workflow. AI-assisted coding tools are now capable of understanding context, suggesting entire feature implementations, and even identifying potential bugs before they reach production. This isn't about replacing developers — it's about augmenting their capabilities and allowing them to focus on the creative and architectural decisions that truly matter.
blogPost.aiLi1: Context-aware code completion that understands your entire codebase
blogPost.aiLi2: Automated code review with security vulnerability detection
blogPost.aiLi3: AI-generated test suites that cover edge cases humans might miss
blogPost.aiLi4: Natural language to code translation for rapid prototyping
blogPost.edgeP: Edge computing has moved from an experimental approach to a mainstream deployment strategy. By running computation closer to users, applications achieve dramatically lower latency and better user experiences. Modern frameworks now support edge-first deployment models that make this approach accessible to every developer.
blogPost.wasmP: WebAssembly continues to break boundaries, enabling near-native performance for complex applications running in the browser. From video editing to 3D modeling to scientific computing, WASM is expanding what's possible on the web platform.
blogPost.bizHeading: What This Means for Your Business
blogPost.bizP: Staying ahead of these trends isn't just about technology for its own sake — it's about delivering better experiences to your users, reducing development costs, and gaining competitive advantages. At TRS, we're committed to leveraging these innovations to help our clients succeed.
blogPost.ctaTitle: Want to discuss how these trends apply to your project?
